script: The real range of the BYD Tang, Tesla Model 3, and Hyundai Ioniq 5 will surprise you. At 50,000 miles, the BYD Tang retains 92% of its battery capacity, while the Tesla Model 3 holds 95%, and the Hyundai Ioniq 5 keeps 94%. In terms of driving range, the BYD Tang delivers 215 miles, the Tesla Model 3 reaches 240 miles, and the Hyundai Ioniq 5 goes for 225 miles. Considering these numbers, would you buy these cars at 50,000 miles? The data suggests they still have a lot to offer. Subscribe to Evcarix for real EV data.
description:
150,000 Mile EVs: Would You Buy These Cars at This Price?

In this video, we dive deep into Would You Buy These Cars at This Many Miles? 🥸. We analyze the data, performance metrics, and technical specifications to see how it performs in real-world conditions. Does the range match the claims? Watch to find out the real numbers behind 150,000 Mile EVs: Would You Buy These Cars at This Price?.

#electricvehicles #highmileageEVs #usedelectriccars #EVbatterylife #150000mileEV #electriccarreliability #longrangeEV #affordableelectricvehicles

Evcarix is a data-driven electric vehicle channel focused on real-world EV performance and battery science.
We test electric cars beyond marketing claims — measuring true driving range, battery efficiency, winter range loss, cold weather performance, charging speed, charging costs, and long-term EV ownership experience.

On this channel you’ll find:
• Real-world EV range tests
• Winter vs summer EV performance comparisons
• EV battery degradation analysis
• LFP vs NMC battery comparisons
• Fast charging impact explained
• EV efficiency & consumption breakdowns
• Electric vehicle technology explained clearly

Our mission is simple: No hype. Just numbers. ⚡

If you care about real electric vehicle data, battery performance, and honest EV analysis — welcome to Evcarix ⚡
